---
title: "XR251_data_explore_dotplot"
author: "Alexander Lercher"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: github_document
---

## LOAD PACKES
```{r load packages}
library(Seurat)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(knitr)
```

## LOAD in SEURAT RDS files from Maria Sierra
```{r load in seurat rds files from Maria Sierra}
data <- readRDS(file = "input/seu_obj_v12192025.RDS")

# rename and plot by sample
table(data@meta.data$orig.ident)
data$condition <-  dplyr::recode(
  data$orig.ident,
  "24177-05-01" = "00_hours",
  "24177-05-02" = "02_hours",
  "24177-05-03" = "16_hours"
)

# rename and plot by sample
table(data@meta.data$manual)
data$manual <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$manual == "VSMCs" ~ "Fibroblast",
  data$manual == "Fibroblast" ~ "Fibroblast-like",
  data$manual == "Ventricular Ephithelial cells" ~ "Ventricular Epithelial cells",
  TRUE ~ as.character(data$manual)              # keep everything else
) %>%
  factor() %>%                                  # convert back to factor
  droplevels()

# rename manual to cell_type (maria called it manual because we annotated manually)
data@meta.data <- data@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::rename(cell_type = manual)

# reset Idents()
Idents(data) <- data$cell_type

#save my modified Seurat object
saveRDS(data, file = "input/seu_obj_v12192025_AL.rds")
```

## INSPECT AND SAVE UMAPs GENERATED BY MARIA
```{r inspect and save UMAPs generated by Maria}
# define colors
cols <- c(
  "#E69F00","#56B4E9","#009E73","#F0E000",
  "#0072B2","#D55E00","#CC79A7","#999999",
  "#1B9E77","#D90F02","#7570B3","#E7298A"
)

# UMAP by cell type
p1 <- DimPlot(data,
              reduction = "umap",
              group.by = "cell_type",
              label = TRUE,
              repel = TRUE,
              cols = cols) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  labs(title = "cell types")

print(p1)
ggsave("output/UMAPs/umap_cell_types.pdf", p1, width = 12, height = 12)

# UMAP split by condition
p2 <- DimPlot(data,
              reduction = "umap",
              split.by = "condition",
              ncol = 3,
              label = TRUE,
              repel = TRUE,
              cols = cols) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  labs(title = "samples")

print(p2)
```

## CALCULATE AND VISUALIZE CELLS PER CLUSTER PER SAMPLE
```{r calculate and visualize cells per cluster per sample}
# summarize cell counts and calculate proportion per cluster per sample
cell_counts <- data@meta.data %>%
  count(cell_type, condition) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = condition,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(total_cells = rowSums(across(where(is.numeric)))) %>%
  mutate(across(
    where(is.numeric),
    ~ .x / total_cells,
    .names = "prop_{.col}"
  ))

cell_counts %>%
  kable(
    format = "markdown",
    digits = 2,
    caption = "Cells per cluster"
  )

write_tsv(cell_counts, "output/cells_per_cluster.tsv")

# plot bar graphs
cell_counts_long <- cell_counts %>%
  dplyr::select(cell_type, prop_00_hours, prop_02_hours, prop_16_hours) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("prop_"),
    names_to = "condition",
    values_to = "proportion"
  ) %>%
  mutate(condition = gsub("prop_", "", condition))

p <- ggplot(cell_counts_long, aes(x = cell_type, y = proportion, fill = condition)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  ylab("Proportion of cells") +
  xlab("Cell type") +
  ggtitle("Cells per cluster per sample") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        aspect.ratio = 1)

print(p)
```

## PLOT MARKER GENES PER CLUSTER
```{r plot marker genes per cluster}
top_markers <- read_csv("input/top20_markers_manual_clusters.csv")
# omit first column (useless)
top_markers <- top_markers %>%
  dplyr::select(-1)

# select top 5 genes per cluster
top5 <- top_markers %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = avg_log2FC, n = 5) %>%
  ungroup()

top5 %>%
  arrange(cluster, desc(avg_log2FC)) %>%
  kable(
    format = "markdown",
    digits = 2,
    caption = "Top 5 marker genes per cluster"
  )

# chose 3 genes per cluster
genesOI <- c("Slc17a7","Satb2","Cadps2", #excitatory neurons
             "Rgs9","Gad2","Drd2", #inhibitory neurons
             "Pvalb","Lhx6","Sst", #interneurons
             "Mag","Mog","Cnp", #oligodendrocytes
             "Pdgfra","Olig1","Sox10", #OPC             
             "Ntsr2","Fgfr3","Aldh1l1", #astrocytes             
             "Ptprc","P2ry12","Siglech", #microglia
             "Cldn5","Pecam1","Kdr", #endothelial             
             "Pdgfrb","Rgs5","Abcc9", #pericytes
             "Col12a1","Adamtsl3","Bnc2", #fibroblast-like
             "Col1a2","Col4a6","Lama1", #fibroblast
             "Gmnc","Slc4a5","Cdh3") #ventricular epithelial

# sort order of cell types in "idents"
new_order <- rev(c("Excitatory neurons",
                "Inhibitory neurons",
                "Interneurons",
                "Oligodendrocytes",
                "OPCs",
                "Astrocytes",
                "Microglia",
                "Endothelial cells",
                "Pericytes",
                "Fibroblast-like",
                "Fibroblast",
                "Ventricular Epithelial cells"))

# reorder cell types acc to marker genes
data_to_plot <- data
Idents(data_to_plot) <- factor(Idents(data_to_plot), levels = new_order)

# print dotplot
p <- DotPlot(data_to_plot, features = genesOI, dot.scale = 10) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightgrey", high = "firebrick",
                       limits = c(1, NA), oob = scales::squish) +
  RotatedAxis() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        aspect.ratio = 0.75)

print(p)
```

## APPENDIX
```{r appendix}
# which R packages and versions?
if ("devtools" %in% installed.packages()) devtools::session_info()
```
